name: Source Fidelity review for Circulating Factor Modulation bundle
summary: The staged source, study, registry, and finding records match the checked source boundaries. PMID 40592961 is represented as healthy-adult plasmapheresis biomarker limiting evidence; PMID 40928812 is represented as Alzheimer disease real-world cohort context; the young-donor plasma exchange item stays preprint safety and feasibility context; the microplastics paper stays short-term circulating-burden biomarker context; and the registry records are no-results or no-registry-results status records.

findings.claim_or_issue: The staged plasmapheresis finding extracts the correct population, protocol boundary, biomarker endpoint, and limiting result from PMID 40592961.
findings.why_it_matters: The repair depends on treating this source as a counterweight to favorable healthy-adult TPE biomarker evidence rather than as another positive rejuvenation signal.
findings.recommended_action: Retain the staged wording that says repeated plasmapheresis changed biomarkers but did not show epigenetic rejuvenation and may have moved several clock measures unfavorably.

findings.claim_or_issue: The staged Alzheimer TPE finding correctly records the treated cohort size, historical-control comparator, MMSE and memory signal, nonsignificant FAQ boundary, and no severe adverse events.
findings.why_it_matters: This can support disease-specific human functional context, but it cannot broaden the track to general healthy-aging functional benefit.
findings.recommended_action: Keep the historical-control and Alzheimer disease caveats attached to the finding and outlook support map.

findings.claim_or_issue: The staged NCT records are handled as trial-status context, while the young-donor plasma MCI preprint is handled as preprint safety and feasibility evidence only.
findings.why_it_matters: No-results registries and preprints should not be counted as efficacy progress.
findings.recommended_action: Retain the staged outlook language that says registry status and preprint evidence need controlled results before affecting the public read.
